axis: interp dispatch arm sweep — audit ALL I/O arms for the read_file+strlen / unsafe-shellout antipattern fixed by ω-stdlib-3
trigger: ω-stdlib-3 (commit 205fa545) fixed file_size dispatch arm; this cycle audits siblings (file_*, dir_*, path_*, read_*, write_*) for the same class of bug.

scope.lines_searched: 9936-15000 (interp builtin dispatch chain — 474 `if name ==` arms total)

arms_audited.issue: dir paths returned BSD `stat -f %z` data-block size (e.g. 10624) instead of 0; mismatched rt_file_size S_ISREG semantics
arms_audited.fix: added `test -f` S_ISREG gate before stat shellout

arms_audited.note: host call; not file I/O size-sensitive
delegates to host rt_read_file with error-tag handling

arms_audited.issue: exec("test -d '"+path+"'") naively concatenates path inside single quotes — paths with apostrophes break the shell parse
same naive single-quote concat as dir_exists
same naive concat; also no stderr swallow
arms_audited.fix: single-quote-escape + 2>/dev/null. Newline-in-filename limitation noted, out of scope

arms_audited.issue: reimplemented as read_file(p).split('\n') — same antipattern as ω-stdlib-3 file_size: whole-file RAM read, drifts from rt_read_lines contract, no try/catch, no host-error handling
arms_audited.fix: delegate to host read_lines with try/catch wrapper (mirrors arm A at 13301)

arms_audited.issue: second copy of arm-A bug (apostrophe quoting)

fixes_applied.before: stat -f %z (BSD) → stat -c %s (GNU) — no S_ISREG gate; directories returned BSD inode-block size (e.g. 10624)
fixes_applied.after: added `test -f` gate before stat — S_ISREG-equivalent; directories now return 0 (matches rt_file_size at runtime.c:3909)
fixes_applied.rationale: ω-stdlib-2 selftest expected dir→0 but stdlib-3 dispatch arm still returned macOS dir-block size. This cycle closes the parity gap.

fixes_applied.before: let content = read_file(p); let lines = content.split("\n")
fixes_applied.after: let lines = read_lines(p) (host call, with try/catch wrapper, mirrors arm A at 13301)
fixes_applied.rationale: Same antipattern as ω-stdlib-3 file_size: re-implements via read_file→whole-file-RAM, drifts from rt_read_lines C-side contract, no error tag handling. Now delegates to host.

fixes_applied.arm: dir_exists / mkdir / list_dir (both copies — arms A at 13314/13322/13329 and arms C at 14641/14651/14660)
fixes_applied.before: exec("test -d '" + path + "'") and exec("mkdir -p '" + path + "'") and exec("ls -1 '" + path + "'") — single-quote concatenation breaks on paths containing an apostrophe
fixes_applied.after: let escaped = "'" + path.replace("'", "'\\''") + "'" before exec; mirrors the escape pattern already used by file_size at 10606. list_dir gained `2>/dev/null` to swallow noise.
fixes_applied.rationale: Matches ω-stdlib-3 audit criterion 'arms that re-implement what a C builtin already does correctly' — these have no C builtin so the shellout stays, but quoting safety must match. Newline-in-filename is a separate limitation tracked but out of scope (would require a real readdir(3) builtin).

covers: read_lines parity on small text file (3 lines)
dir_exists/mkdir/list_dir on a path with embedded apostrophe ("/tmp/_omega_stdlib_4_d'ir") — survives the quote-escape

regression.improvement: directory file_size now returns 0 (was 10624 on macOS via BSD stat -f %z)

scope_constraints_honored: no anima edits (selftest in /tmp not anima/tool)
no docker/Linux ELF rebuild — Darwin host interp only
rollback: git checkout -- /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/self/hexa_full.hexa && ./hexa tool/build_interp.hexa  # reverts: file_size S_ISREG gate, read_lines arm B host-delegation, dir_exists/mkdir/list_dir quote escape (both copies)
closure_status: COMPLETE — 28 I/O arms audited, 8 fixed (1 file_size S_ISREG, 1 read_lines re-impl, 6 quote-escapes across 3 fns × 2 dispatch copies), 0 deferred. Selftests PASS.  Bonus: stdlib-2 directory selftest regression also resolved by file_size S_ISREG gate.
